Title: The target of rapamycin kinase is a positive regulator of plant fatty acid and lipid synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression under Torin 2 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcript abundance decreased when TOR was inhibited, consistent with reduced fatty acid and lipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACC2 was identified as a lipid-metabolism gene downregulated after 1 d Torin 2 treatment. 2. Its repression is consistent with impaired de novo fatty acid synthesis under TOR inhibition. 3. The paper does not directly functionally validate ACC2 beyond transcript profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as downstream TOR-responsive enzyme gene, not the primary experimental focal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcript abundance decreased upon TOR inhibition, consistent with reduced de novo fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAB2 was significantly downregulated after 8 h of Torin 2 treatment. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed FAB2 repression. 3. The result supports TOR-promoted expression of de novo fatty acid synthesis genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream TOR-responsive pathway gene; not directly manipulated genetically in this study.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of fatty acid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcript abundance decreased upon TOR inhibition, consistent with reduced fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KASI was significantly downregulated in Torin 2-treated seedlings. 2. RT-qPCR validated KASI repression. 3. This supports the conclusion that TOR positively regulates de novo fatty acid synthesis gene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream TOR-responsive pathway gene; not primary experimental focal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid fatty acid synthase carrier module
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of fatty acid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transcript abundance decreased upon TOR inhibition, consistent with reduced de novo fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACP transcript was significantly reduced by Torin 2 treatment. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed ACP repression. 3. The result aligns with lower de novo fatty acid synthesis under TOR suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carrier protein rather than catalytic enzyme in strict sense, but listed by the paper among de novo fatty acid synthesis genes.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Target of rapamycin Ser/Thr protein kinase, PIKK family
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana; Brassica napus
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis Columbia-0; Brassica napus cv. Jet Neuf
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient TOR-GFP expression in N. benthamiana; estradiol-inducible RNAi system for tor-es1 in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: widely expressed across different plant tissues
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: TOR complex with LST8-1 and RAPTOR1B
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: light; sugars; energy availability; nutritional status; environmental cues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: total fatty acids; membrane lipids; triacylglycerol
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane biogenesis for cell growth; energy storage
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid synthesis and lipid turnover balance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ACC2; FAB2; KASI; ACP; MAGL; TAGL; PLA and other lipid metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive regulation of fatty acid/lipid synthesis gene expression and negative regulation of lipid turnover gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates/upregulates de novo fatty acid synthesis genes and represses/downregulates lipase genes involved in lipid turnover
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator of plant fatty acid and lipid synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Torin 2 inhibitor treatment; estradiol-induced TOR RNAi; transient TOR overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression; inducible RNAi silencing; chemical inhibition with Torin 2; TFA/TAG quantification; lipidomics; 14C-acetate labeling; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TOR promotes accumulation of total fatty acids, membrane lipids, and TAG; TOR inhibition reduces these lipid pools and de novo fatty acid synthesis rate
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transient TOR overexpression in N. benthamiana increased total fatty acids by about 27%. 2. TOR silencing or Torin 2 inhibition in Arabidopsis reduced total fatty acids and membrane lipids and lowered de novo fatty acid synthesis. 3. Torin 2 treatment in Brassica napus suspension cells reduced both total fatty acids and TAG accumulation. 4. RNA-seq showed TOR positively regulates expression of de novo fatty acid synthesis genes while repressing lipid turnover genes.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq on Arabidopsis seedlings treated with 1 μM Torin 2 for 8 h or 1 d, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Prolonged TOR suppression in prior studies can cause stress-related TAG accumulation in vegetative tissues; TOR broadly controls growth-related processes so manipulation may have pleiotropic effects.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance fatty acid and lipid production, but because TOR is a central growth regulator, engineering should favor context-specific or moderate activation rather than constitutive strong perturbation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRI1 was discussed but was not differentially expressed, suggesting TOR-mediated lipid regulation likely functions in parallel to WRI1. The study emphasizes TOR as the primary focal gene; downstream lipid genes were transcriptional responses rather than core experimental subjects.
